one_liner: NLP SelfCheckGPT hallucination detection to medical VLM radiology report reliability
problem: Med-VLMs generate hallucinated findings but there is no systematic post-hoc hallucination detection pipeline for medical reports; NLP consistency sampling has never been ported to report generation.
approach: 1.Multi-sample Med-VLM outputs on CheXpert compute sentence-level consistency via BERTScore and NLI entailment. 2.Verify low-consistency sentences mismatch image-level annotations. 3.Compare against RADRAG retrieval-augmented baseline.
why_new: SelfCheckGPT (Manakul 2023) is SOTA for LLM hallucination; medical report side has only RadFlag 2024 black-box method; nobody uses consistency sampling directly and RadFlag does not quantify image-text inconsistency.
source_method: LLM hallucination detection SelfCheckGPT (Manakul et al. 2023); consistency sampling

one_liner: Causal Rosenbaum sensitivity analysis to medical AI OOD uncertainty decomposition
problem: Medical AI is sensitive to scanner/population shift but we cannot determine how much of the confidence drop is caused by unobserved confounding. Rosenbaum sensitivity analysis never applied to deep medical imaging models.
approach: 1.Train chest X-ray classifier on NIH-CXR14 test on VinDr-CXR. 2.Apply Rosenbaum sensitivity analysis compute Gamma value. 3.Use scanner metadata causal graph to validate Gamma matches actual confounding strength.
why_new: Rosenbaum sensitivity analysis standard in epidemiology; deep learning OOD evaluation has never used it. Existing domain adaptation does not ask how strong confounding must be to cause failure.
source_method: causal sensitivity analysis (Rosenbaum and Rubin 1983; Ding and VanderWeele 2016 E-value)

one_liner: Meteorological CRPS proper scoring rule to medical segmentation UQ evaluation replacing ECE
problem: ECE has high binning variance and is semantically blind in pixel-level segmentation. CRPS is the meteorological gold standard proper scoring rule and has never been used for segmentation UQ evaluation protocols.
approach: 1.Run MC-Dropout deep ensemble and temperature scaling on BraTS and Camelyon16 replacing ECE with CRPS. 2.Compare CRPS vs DSC-calibration ranking consistency. 3.Propose spatial-CRPS variant weighting boundary regions.
why_new: CRPS (Gneiting and Raftery 2007 JASA) standard in meteorology; medical segmentation calibration rarely uses it. UNSURE 2023 survey still only recommends ECE/reliability diagram. Novelty: stricter proper score reevaluates existing methods without model changes.
source_method: meteorological proper scoring rules CRPS (Gneiting and Raftery 2007 JASA)

one_liner: Financial CVaR Expected Shortfall to tail error risk quantification for medical AI models
problem: Medical AI reports mean AUC masking tail extreme errors in the worst 5% of samples. Financial CVaR for tail risk quantification has never been applied to medical AI deployment risk assessment.
approach: 1.Group predictions by confidence for UQ methods on HAM10000/CheXpert compute tail CVaR error rate. 2.Verify high-CVaR models cause greater clinical tail harm even with similar mean AUC. 3.Propose CVaR as model selection criterion.
why_new: CVaR (Rockafellar and Uryasev 2000) and DRO mature in finance/robust ML; medical AI evaluation has zero CVaR usage. Unlike MedAD-FailMap per-image conspicuity this targets classifier tail not reconstruction-based AD: no overlap.
source_method: financial CVaR Expected Shortfall (Rockafellar and Uryasev 2000; DRO Duchi 2019)
